completeness.rationale: The triage captured most expected information: crash on manual save (toolbar button), related to CSV import, encoding error message, and that removing imported tasks fixes it. However, it missed two items: the agent didn't ask about or discover the auto-save distinction (that auto-save works fine), and didn't collect environment details (macOS 14.2, TaskFlow v2.3.1). The size threshold (works under 50 tasks) was also not explored. The summary notes ~200 tasks but doesn't identify the size dependency.

accuracy.rationale: The root cause hypothesis is close to the actual cause — it correctly identifies encoding/special characters in CSV-imported data causing the save serializer to crash. It misses the payload size threshold (64KB) as a contributing factor and doesn't identify that the app panics rather than handling errors gracefully, though it does recommend defensive handling. The general direction is correct and would lead a developer to the right area of code.

efficiency.rationale: The conversation completed in just 2 agent questions, which is very efficient. Both questions were well-targeted and elicited substantial information. The agent could have asked one more question about environment details and whether smaller lists also crash, but the brevity is a strength given how much was extracted.

question_quality.rationale: The first question effectively narrowed down the save context, and the reporter volunteered the CSV connection. The second question smartly asked about error messages and persistence across restarts, which drew out the 'encoding' clue and the key diagnostic finding that removing imported tasks fixes it. The questions showed good diagnostic reasoning. A follow-up about environment or list size would have been ideal.

actionability.rationale: The summary provides clear reproduction steps, a solid root cause hypothesis pointing to encoding issues in the CSV import/save pipeline, and specific recommended fixes. A developer could start investigating the save serializer's encoding handling immediately. The missing size threshold detail means they might not immediately find the 64KB boundary, but they'd be looking in the right place.

notable_strengths: Extracted the critical CSV import connection and encoding error in only 2 turns
Root cause hypothesis is well-reasoned and close to the actual cause
Recommended fix covers both import-time sanitization and save-time defensive handling
The second question was well-structured, combining error message and persistence inquiries to maximize information per turn
notable_weaknesses: Failed to collect environment details (OS version, app version) which were expected extracts
Did not explore the size dependency — smaller lists save fine even with imported data
Did not discover or ask about auto-save working fine vs manual save crashing
Summary states 'Not platform-specific' without evidence, when environment info was simply never collected
most_insightful_question: When the crash happens, do you see any error message or dialog before the app closes? — This drew out the crucial 'encoding' clue that anchored the root cause hypothesis.
missed_opportunities: Should have asked about the app version and OS to complete environment details
Should have asked whether auto-save also triggers the crash or only the manual Save button
Should have explored whether the crash depends on list size (e.g., does a small import also crash?)
Could have asked what kinds of data were in the CSV to identify special characters as the trigger
